title: Antony and Cleopatra

What are the main themes of Antony and Cleopatra?
The main themes of Antony and Cleopatra include love vs. duty, empire, honour, self-destruction, and political power.
What does Antony and Cleopatra explore?
Antony and Cleopatra explores themes of love vs. duty, empire, honour, self-destruction, and political power.
Is love vs. duty a theme in Antony and Cleopatra?
Yes, love vs. duty is a central theme in Antony and Cleopatra.
Is empire a theme in Antony and Cleopatra?
Yes, empire is a central theme in Antony and Cleopatra.
Is honour a theme in Antony and Cleopatra?
Yes, honour is a central theme in Antony and Cleopatra.
Why does Antony marry Octavia?
Antony marries Octavia as a political alliance with Octavius, though his heart remains with Cleopatra.
How does Antony die?
Antony stabs himself after receiving a false report that Cleopatra is dead, then dies in her arms.
How does Cleopatra die?
Cleopatra kills herself with an asp to avoid being paraded as a captive in Rome.
What is the central conflict in Antony and Cleopatra?
Antony must choose between his duty as a Roman triumvir and his love for Cleopatra, Queen of Egypt.
Why does Enobarbus desert Antony?
Enobarbus deserts after Antony's judgment is compromised by passion, but dies of grief over his betrayal.
How does Antony and Cleopatra end?
Both Antony and Cleopatra die; Octavius becomes the sole ruler of Rome.
What is the ending of Antony and Cleopatra?
Both Antony and Cleopatra die; Octavius becomes the sole ruler of Rome.
Why is Antony and Cleopatra a tragedy?
Antony and Cleopatra is a tragedy because both antony and cleopatra die; octavius becomes the sole ruler of rome.
Who says "Age cannot wither her, nor custom stale her infinite variety." in Antony and Cleopatra?
Enobarbus says this famous line in Antony and Cleopatra.
What is a famous line from Antony and Cleopatra?
A famous line from Antony and Cleopatra is: "Age cannot wither her, nor custom stale her infinite variety."
